seoTitle: How to Make Your Wix Website Multilingual
metaDescription: Learn how to activate the Wix Multilingual app, auto-translate your site, and refine translations for a global audience.
intro: Expanding your Wix site to reach international visitors starts with the Wix Multilingual app, which lets you add secondary languages and auto-translate your content in bulk. This guide covers setting your primary and secondary languages, using machine translation as a starting point, and manually refining the results before adding a language switcher to your header.

steps.heading: Open your site settings
steps.body: From your Wix dashboard, navigate to the Settings panel to find localization options.

steps.heading: Enable Multilingual
steps.body: Locate the Multilingual toggle and launch the core language setup interface.

steps.heading: Set your languages
steps.body: Define your primary language and add the secondary languages you want to support.

steps.heading: Run auto-translation
steps.body: Activate the automated machine translation tool to convert your content in bulk as a starting point.

steps.heading: Manually refine translations
steps.body: Switch between localized page views to correct specific text blocks, button labels, and swap any media that needs localization.

steps.heading: Add a language selector
steps.body: Embed a language selector widget in your site header and preview the multi-language navigation before publishing.

faq.question: Is machine translation accurate enough to publish as-is?
faq.answer: It's a solid starting point, but manually reviewing and refining text, especially idioms and marketing copy, is recommended before publishing.

faq.question: Can visitors switch languages themselves?
faq.answer: Yes, once you add the language selector widget to your header, visitors can toggle between your supported languages.

faq.question: Does multilingual setup affect my SEO?
faq.answer: Each language version can have its own metadata, which is worth setting up individually to support search visibility in each language.

faq.question: Can I translate images and buttons too, not just text?
faq.answer: Yes, the multilingual editor lets you swap media assets and button text independently for each language version.
